free_text: Proton Elastic Scattering Measurements at 16 MeV with
Optical-Model Analysis.
Last checking has been done.
Ni-58,Ni-60,Ni-62,Ni-64,Zr-90,Sn-120 and Pb-208 were
Used As Target.Target Thicknesses Ranged from 500 Mug/
Cm**2 to 2.5 Mg/Cm**2,and Isotopically Enriched Target
Materials was Used in All Cases.All Ni Isotope Target
And Zr-90 were Mounted As Self-Supporting Metal Foils,
While Sn-120 and Pb-208 were Made by Evaporation
Techniques Using a Carbon Backing.
.Optical Model Calculations.
.A Two 1-Mm Silicon Surface-Barrier Detectors
And the Monitor Detectors (2- AND 3-MM THICK LITHIUM-
DRIFTED SILICON DETECTORS).
*En-Rsl* the Typical Resolution was About 60 KeV.
.The Total Charge was Recorded by a Beam Current
Integrator Accurate to +-1%.
The Absolute Normalizations For the Data were
Determined Expermentally by Measuring the Scattering at
Forward Angles For Proton Energies Around 4 MeV and
Assuming this Obeys a Rutherford Law.
.The Uncertainty is Reported by Authors.
.The Uncertainty is Reported by Authors.
.Error in Angular Setting of the Detectors.
.Error in a Beam Current.
.The Dead-Time Corrections.
.Error in the Target Thickness.
